goal: Generate a benchmark-stable vtk.js scene that can seed later task-specific benchmark cases
title: vtk.js Scene Validation
abstract: Generate vtk.js code for the requested scene while preserving a deterministic browser-validation path. Scene kind: benchmark. Use these retrieval-backed patterns: Use slice, volume, streamline, and mag-iso as the benchmark backbone for vtk.js eval. | Seed streamline scenes explicitly and confirm vector-field inputs before validation. | Use a minimal sphere or cone scene to re-establish canvas rendering before restoring custom logic. | Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict. | Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation. | Inject the vtk.js browser bundle before any module script that references global vtk.. Avoid regressions captured by these failure-fix hints: Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable. | If streamline output is empty, inspect seed generation and pipeline ordering before general browser repair. | If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts. | If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command. | When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic. | If slice rendering regresses, compare mapper wiring and viewport setup against a minimal slice baseline.. Acceptance contract: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification. | The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render(). | Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png. | The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors. | The generated case should stay deterministic enough for later benchmark comparison..
